title: देवी माहात्म्यम् प्रथम चरित्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री देवी माहात्म्यम् - प्रथम चरित्रम् ॥

ॐ नमश्चण्डिकायै॥

ध्यानम् -
खड्गं चक्रगदेषुचापपरिघाञ्छूलं भुशुण्डीं शिरः
शंखं संदधतीं करैस्त्रिनयनां सर्वाङ्गभूषावृताम्।
नीलाश्मद्युतिमास्यपाददशकां सेवे महाकालिकां
यामस्तौत्स्वपिते हरौ कमलजो हन्तुं मधुं कैटभम्॥

ॐ ऐं मार्कण्डेय उवाच -

सावर्णिः सूर्यतनयो यो मनुः कथ्यतेऽष्टमः।
निशामय तदुत्पत्तिं विस्तराद्गदतो मम॥१॥

महामायानुभावेन यथा मन्वन्तराधिपः।
स बभूव महाभागः सावर्णिस्तनयो रवेः॥२॥

स्वारोचिषेऽन्तरे पूर्वं चैत्रवंशसमुद्भवः।
सुरथो नाम राजाभूत्समस्ते क्षितिमण्डले॥३॥

तस्य पालयतः सम्यक् प्रजाः पुत्रानिवौरसान्।
बभूवुः शत्रवो भूपाः कोलाविध्वंसिनस्तदा॥४॥

तस्य तैरभवद्युद्धमतिप्रबलदण्डिनः।
न्यूनैरपि स तैर्युद्धे कोलाविध्वंसिभिर्जितः॥५॥

ततः स्वपुरमायातो निजदेशाधिपोऽभवत्।
आक्रान्तः स महाभागस्तैस्तदा प्रबलारिभिः॥६॥

अमात्यैर्बलिभिर्दुष्टैर्दुर्बलस्य दुरात्मभिः।
कोशो बलं चापहृतं तत्रापि स्वपुरे ततः॥७॥

ततो मृगयाव्याजेन हृतस्वाम्यः स भूपतिः।
एकाकी हयमारुह्य जगाम गहनं वनम्॥८॥

स तत्राश्रममद्राक्षीद्द्विजवर्यस्य मेधसः।
प्रशान्तश्वापदाकीर्णं मुनिशिष्योपशोभितम्॥९॥

तस्मिन् स कतिचित्कालं मुनिना सह सोऽवसत्॥१०॥

स चिन्तयामास ततः ममत्वाकृष्टमानसः।
मत्पूर्वैः पालितं पूर्वं मया हीनं पुरं हि तत्॥११॥

मद्भृत्यैस्तैरसद्वृत्तैर्धर्मतः पाल्यते न वा।
न जाने मम पूर्वैः सः शस्तो वाजी सदामदः॥१२॥

वैश्यश्च समुपस्थित एकं चिन्तापरं ध्रुवम्।
राजोवाच - भवान्कोऽत्र किमर्थमागतोऽत्र च॥१३॥

वैश्य उवाच -
समाधिर्नाम वैश्योऽहं बधूपुत्रैर्निराकृतः।
धनलोभादसद्भिश्च हीनो बान्धवसम्पदा॥१४॥

वनमभ्यागतो दुःखी निरस्तश्चाप्तबन्धुभिः।
सोऽहं न वेद्मि पुत्राणां कुशलाकुशलात्मिकाम्॥१५॥

ऋषिरुवाच -
ज्ञानिनामपि चेतांसि देवी भगवती हि सा।
बलादाकृष्य मोहाय महामाया प्रयच्छति॥१६॥

तया विसृज्यते विश्वं सा जगद्धात्री जगत्प्रसूः।
तया विमोहिता एते ज्ञानं यच्छति मुक्तये॥१७॥

महाविद्या महामाया महामेधा महास्मृतिः।
महामोहा च भवती महादेवी महासुरी॥१८॥

तयैतत् प्रेर्यते विश्वमनन्तं सुरमानवैः।
संस्तूयते च भक्त्या तु ब्रह्मणा हरिणा हरेण च॥१९॥

॥ मधुकैटभवधप्रसङ्गः ॥

ब्रह्मा उवाच -

विश्वेश्वरीं जगद्धात्रीं स्थितिसंहारकारिणीम्।
निद्रां भगवतीं विष्णोरतुलां तेजसः प्रभुम्॥२०॥

त्वं स्वाहा त्वं स्वधा त्वं हि वषट्कारः स्वरात्मिका।
सुधा त्वमक्षरे नित्ये त्रिधा मात्रात्मिका स्थिता॥२१॥

अर्धमात्रास्थिता नित्या यानुच्चार्या विशेषतः।
त्वमेव सन्ध्या सावित्री त्वं देवी जननी परा॥२२॥

त्वयैतद्धार्यते विश्वं त्वयैतत्सृज्यते जगत्।
त्वयैतत्पाल्यते देवी त्वमत्स्यन्ते च सर्वदा॥२३॥

विसृष्टौ सृष्टिरूपा त्वं स्थितिरूपा च पालने।
तथा संहृतिरूपान्ते जगतोऽस्य जगन्मयि॥२४॥

त्वं महाविद्या महामाया महामेधा महास्मृतिः।
महामोहा च भवती महादेवी महेश्वरी॥२५॥

एवं स्तुता देवी तामसी तत्र वेधसा।
विष्णोः प्रबोधनार्थाय निहन्तुं मधुकैटभौ॥२६॥

नेत्रासिनासिकाबाहुहृदयेभ्यस्ततोऽखिलात्।
निर्गम्य दर्शने तस्थौ ब्रह्मणोऽव्यक्तजन्मनः॥२७॥

उत्तस्थौ च जगन्नाथस्तया मुक्तो जनार्दनः।
ततो युद्धमभूत्ताभ्यां पञ्चवर्षसहस्रकम्॥२८॥

तावप्युद्धतबलौ मधुकैटभौ तदा।
महामायाविमोहितौ वरं ददतुरव्ययम्॥२९॥

श्रीभगवानुवाच -
ममैवेह वधं यातं यावत् किंचिज्जलं स्थले।

ततस्तयोः परं तेजः पश्यतो विश्वचक्षुषोः।
ऊरुद्वयं विनिश्चिक्ये विष्णुना चक्रधारिणा॥३०॥

॥ इति श्री मार्कण्डेयपुराणे देवीमाहात्म्ये प्रथमचरित्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Devi Mahatmyam - Prathama Charitram ||

Om Namashchandikayai.

Dhyanam -
Khadgam Chakra-gadeshuchapa-parighan Chhulam Bhushundim Shirah
Shankham Sandadhatim Karais-trinayanam Sarvanga-bhushavritam.
Nilashma-dyutim Asya-pada-dashakam Seve Mahakalikam
Yam Astaut Svapite Harau Kamalajo Hantum Madhum Kaitabham.

Om Aim Markandeya Uvacha -

Savarnih Surya-tanayo Yo Manuh Kathyate'shtamah.
Nishamaya Tad-utpattim Vistarad Gadato Mama.||1||

Mahamaya-anubhavena Yatha Manvantaradhipah.
Sa Babhuva Mahabhagah Savarnis Tanayo Raveh.||2||

Svarochishe'ntare Purvam Chaitra-vamsha-samudbhavah.
Suratho Nama Rajabhut Samaste Kshiti-mandale.||3||

Tasya Palayatah Samyak Prajah Putran Ivaurasaan.
Babhuvuh Shatravo Bhupah Kolavidhvamsinas Tada.||4||

Tasya Tair Abhavad Yuddham Ati-prabala-dandinah.
Nyunair Api Sa Tair Yuddhe Kolavidhvamsibhir Jitah.||5||

Tatah Svapuram Ayato Nija-deshadhipo'bhavat.
Aakrantah Sa Mahabhaagas Tais Tada Prabalaaribhih.||6||

Amatyair Balibhir Dushtair Durbalasya Duratmabhih.
Kosho Balam Chapahritam Tatrapi Svapure Tatah.||7||

Tato Mrigaya-vyajena Hrita-svamyah Sa Bhupatih.
Ekaki Hayam Aruhya Jagama Gahanam Vanam.||8||

Sa Tatrashramam Adrakshid Dvija-varyasya Medhasah.
Prashanta-shvapada-akirnam Muni-shishyopashobhitam.||9||

Tasmin Sa Katichit Kalam Munina Saha So'vasat.||10||

Sa Chintayamasa Tatah Mamatva-akrishta-manasah.
Mat-purvaih Palitam Purvam Maya Hinam Puram Hi Tat.||11||

Mad-bhrityais Tair Asad-vrittair Dharmatah Palyate Na Va.
Na Jane Mama Purvaih Sah Shasto Vaji Sada-madah.||12||

Vaishyas Cha Samupasthita Ekam Chintaparam Dhruvam.
Raja Uvacha - Bhavan Ko'tra Kim-artham Agato'tra Cha.||13||

Vaishya Uvacha -
Samadhir Nama Vaishyo'ham Badhu-putrair Nirakritah.
Dhana-lobhad Asadbhis Cha Hino Bandhava-sampada.||14||

Vanam Abhyagato Duhkhi Nirastas Chapta-bandhubhih.
So'ham Na Vedmi Putranam Kushala-akushalatmikam.||15||

Rishir Uvacha -
Jnaninam Api Chetamsi Devi Bhagavati Hi Sa.
Balad Akrishya Mohaya Mahamaya Prayachchhati.||16||

Taya Visrijyate Vishvam Sa Jagaddhatri Jagat-prasuh.
Taya Vimohita Ete Jnanam Yachchhati Muktaye.||17||

Mahavidya Mahamaya Mahamedha Mahasmritih.
Mahamoha Cha Bhavati Mahadevi Mahasuri.||18||

Tayaitat Preryate Vishvam Anantam Sura-maanavaih.
Samstuyate Cha Bhaktya Tu Brahmana Harina Harena Cha.||19||

|| Madhu-Kaitabha-vadha-prasangah ||

Brahma Uvacha -
Vishveshvarim Jagaddhatrim Sthiti-samhara-karinim.
Nidram Bhagavatim Vishnor Atulam Tejasah Prabhum.||20||

Tvam Svaha Tvam Svadha Tvam Hi Vashatkarah Svaratmika.
Sudha Tvam Akshare Nitye Tridha Matraatmika Sthita.||21||

Ardha-matrasthita Nitya Yanuchcharya Visheshatah.
Tvam Eva Sandhya Savitri Tvam Devi Janani Para.||22||

Tvayaitad Dharyate Vishvam Tvayaitat Srijyate Jagat.
Tvayaitat Palyate Devi Tvam Atsyante Cha Sarvada.||23||

Visrishtau Srishti-rupa Tvam Sthiti-rupa Cha Palane.
Tatha Samhriti-rupante Jagato'sya Jaganmayi.||24||

Tvam Mahavidya Mahamaya Mahamedha Mahasmritih.
Mahamoha Cha Bhavati Mahadevi Maheshvari.||25||

Evam Stuta Devi Tamasi Tatra Vedhasa.
Vishnoh Prabodhanarthaya Nihantum Madhu-Kaitabhau.||26||

Netrasi-nasika-bahu-hridayebhyas Tato'khilat.
Nirgamya Darshane Tasthau Brahmano'vyakta-janmanah.||27||

Uttasthau Cha Jagannathah Taya Mukto Janardanah.
Tato Yuddham Abhut Tabhyam Pancha-varsha-sahasrakam.||28||

Tav Apy Uddhata-balau Madhu-Kaitabhau Tada.
Mahamaya-vimohitau Varam Dadatur Avyayam.||29||

Shri Bhagavan Uvacha -
Mamaiveha Vadham Yatam Yavat Kinchij Jalam Sthale.

Tatas Tayoh Param Tejah Pashyato Vishva-chakshushoh.
Uru-dvayam Vinishchikye Vishnuna Chakra-dharina.||30||

|| Iti Shri Markandeya-purane Devi-mahatmye Prathama-charitram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
मार्कण्डेय ऋषि ने कहा - सूर्य के पुत्र सावर्णि जो आठवें मनु कहे जाते हैं, उनकी उत्पत्ति की कथा विस्तार से सुनो।

पूर्वकाल में स्वारोचिष मन्वन्तर में चैत्रवंश में सुरथ नाम के राजा हुए जो समस्त पृथ्वी के स्वामी थे। उनके शत्रु कोलाविध्वंसी राजाओं ने उन्हें युद्ध में हरा दिया। दुष्ट अमात्यों ने उनका कोष और बल छीन लिया। वे अकेले घने वन में गए।

वहाँ उन्हें मेधस मुनि का आश्रम मिला। वहीं समाधि नामक वैश्य भी आया जो पुत्रों और स्त्री द्वारा धन के लोभ से निकाला गया था।

ऋषि ने कहा - वह भगवती देवी महामाया ज्ञानियों के भी चित्त को बलपूर्वक मोह में डालती है। वही जगद्धात्री विश्व की सृष्टि, पालन और संहार करती है। वह महाविद्या, महामाया, महामेधा, महास्मृति है।

तब ब्रह्मा ने विष्णु को जगाने के लिए योगनिद्रा की स्तुति की - हे देवी! तुम स्वाहा हो, स्वधा हो, वषट्कार हो। तुम्हीं से यह विश्व धारण, सृजन और पालन होता है।

देवी ने विष्णु को छोड़ा, विष्णु ने पाँच हजार वर्ष युद्ध कर महामाया से मोहित मधु-कैटभ का अपनी जंघाओं पर वध किया।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीप्रथमचरित्रस्य ब्रह्मा ऋषिः। महाकाली देवता। गायत्री छन्दः। नन्दा शक्तिः। रक्तदन्तिका बीजम्। अग्निस्तत्त्वम्। ऋग्वेदः स्वरूपम्। श्रीमहाकालीप्रीत्यर्थे प्रथमचरित्रपाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Narrates the cosmic origin story of Devi's first manifestation as Mahakali
Recitation destroys tamas (ignorance) and awakens higher consciousness
Invokes protection from powerful enemies and adversaries
Traditionally recited for removal of the veil of mahamaya and grants spiritual awakening
Part of the sacred Durga Saptashati recitation for Navratri
Invokes Brahma's praise of Yoganidra for divine intervention
description: Dedicated to Goddess Durga, the Devi Mahatmyam Prathama Charitra (देवी माहात्म्यम् प्रथम चरित्रम्) is a sacred prayer revered in Hindu tradition. Rooted in Markandeya Purana (Devi Mahatmyam / Durga Saptashati), this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to narrate the cosmic origin story of Devi's first manifestation as Mahakali, part of the sacred Durga Saptashati recitation for Navratri, and invoke Brahma's praise of Yoganidra for divine intervention. It is especially recommended on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri for maximum spiritual benefit. At 30 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 20 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Devi Mahatmyam Prathama Charitra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Devi Mahatmyam Prathama Charitra (Chapter 1) in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. The Madhu-Kaitabha story from Durga Saptashati, Markandeya Purana.
relatedSearches: Devi Mahatmyam Prathama Charitra Sanskrit Hindi PDF
Durga Saptashati Chapter 1 Madhu Kaitabha story
King Suratha Samadhi Vaishya Medha Rishi story
Yoganidra stuti Brahma to Vishnu meaning
Mahamaya Vishnu Maya verse Devi Mahatmya
Markandeya Purana Devi Mahatmya origin story
Prathama Charitra benefits for moha removal wisdom

seoFaqs.question: What is the Devi Mahatmyam Prathama Charitra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Devi Mahatmyam Prathama Charitra (देवी माहात्म्यम् प्रथम चरित्रम् — 'the First Episode') is the opening section of the Devi Mahatmya / Durga Saptashati / Chandi Path, comprising chapter 1 of the 13-chapter text preserved in the Markandeya Purana. It contains the frame-narrative of the entire Saptashati: the deposed king Suratha and the dispossessed merchant Samadhi seek refuge in the hermitage of sage Medha, who reveals to them the Devi Mahatmya — the supreme glory of the Goddess. Within this frame, sage Medha narrates the first cosmic Devi-charitra: how Brahma, threatened by the asuras Madhu and Kaitabha during the cosmic dissolution, awakens Yoganidra (the cosmic sleep / Maha-maya) on Vishnu so that Vishnu can rise and slay them. The presiding deity of this charitra is Mahakali, the rishi is Brahma, the chhand is Gayatri, the bija is Raktadantika, the shakti is Nanda, the tattva is Agni, and the svarupa is the Rigveda. (Source: Markandeya Purana, Devi Mahatmya chapter 1.)

seoFaqs.question: Who are King Suratha and the Vaishya Samadhi in the Prathama Charitra?
seoFaqs.answer: King Suratha was a noble king of the Chaitra dynasty in the Svarochisha Manvantara who ruled the entire earth. He was defeated in battle by enemy Kola-vidhvamsi kings and further betrayed by his own corrupt ministers who looted his treasury and army. Heartbroken, Suratha rode alone into the deep forest and reached the hermitage of sage Medha. There he met Samadhi, a wealthy Vaishya merchant who had been driven out of his own home by his greedy wife and sons after losing his wealth. Both — though they had been ill-treated and ruined — found themselves still mentally attached to those who had wronged them, unable to free themselves from mamatva (egoistic attachment). They asked sage Medha why this was so. Medha replied that this is the operation of Devi Maha-maya, who pulls even the wisest minds into delusion — and then proceeded to narrate the three Devi-charitras to liberate them. After hearing the entire Saptashati, both performed Devi tapas; Suratha was reborn as the eighth Manu Savarni, and Samadhi attained moksha. (Source: Devi Mahatmya 1.4-15 and chapter 13.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Madhu-Kaitabha episode narrated in this charitra?
seoFaqs.answer: At the end of the cosmic dissolution (mahapralaya), Vishnu lay in yoga-nidra on Shesha-naga in the cosmic ocean. From the wax of Vishnu's ears two terrible asuras — Madhu and Kaitabha — were born, and they immediately advanced to kill Brahma, who was seated on the lotus growing from Vishnu's navel. Brahma, seeing no escape, recognized that Vishnu's sleep was itself the great cosmic Yoga-nidra, the goddess Maha-maya, holding Vishnu in trance. Brahma offered the celebrated Yoga-nidra Stuti — Vishvesvarim jagaddhatrim sthiti-samhara-karinim — declaring her as Svaha, Svadha, Vashatkara, the eternal akshara, the ardha-matra, Sandhya, Savitri, Janani-para, the supreme Maha-vidya. Pleased, Devi withdrew herself from Vishnu's eyes, nose, mouth, arms and heart. Vishnu awoke and battled Madhu-Kaitabha for 5,000 years. Maha-maya bewildered the asuras into pride, so they granted Vishnu a boon — at which Vishnu asked for their own death. Vishnu then slew them on his thighs (uru-dvayam) using the Sudarshana chakra. (Source: Devi Mahatmya 1.49-87.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Prathama Charitra be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Prathama Charitra is recited daily during Sharad Navratri and Chaitra Navratri — traditionally on day 1 of the nine-day paath schedule, followed by the Madhyama Charitra over days 2-3 and the Uttara Charitra over days 4-9. It is especially powerful on Pratipada (the first tithi of Navratri), on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar), on every Krishna Chaturdashi (sacred to Mahakali), on Kalashtami, on Mahalaya Amavasya, on Dipavali Amavasya (Kali Puja in Bengal), and on solar/lunar eclipses for siddhi. The dhyana invokes the ten-faced, ten-footed, neel-ashma-dyuti Mahakali holding sword, chakra, gada, arrow, bow, parigha, shula, bhushundi, severed head and shankha — recited at dawn after a bath, facing east, before a Mahakali yantra or image with red hibiscus flowers, kumkum and a ghee diya. The Kavach, Argala and Kilaka of the Saptashati must be recited first, then the Navarna mantra, then the Prathama Charitra. (Sources: Devi Mahatmya paath vidhi; Markandeya Purana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the spiritual teaching of the Prathama Charitra?
seoFaqs.answer: The central teaching of the Prathama Charitra is that even the most learned and dispassionate beings — kings, sages, jnanis — remain bound by Devi Maha-maya so long as her grace is not invoked. Sage Medha declares: jnaninam api chetamsi devi bhagavati hi sa, balad akrishya mohaya mahamaya prayachchhati — 'even the minds of the wise, that very Devi Bhagavati Maha-maya forcibly drags into delusion'. The same Devi who binds in samsara is the very one who, when worshipped with bhakti, grants jnana for mukti — taya vimohitah ete jnanam yachchhati muktaye. She is Maha-vidya, Maha-maya, Maha-medha, Maha-smriti, Maha-moha, Maha-devi, Maha-suri — both the binder and the liberator, both yoga-nidra (cosmic sleep) and bodha (awakening). The cosmological Madhu-Kaitabha story is the macrocosmic mirror of the microcosmic predicament of Suratha and Samadhi: just as Devi awakens Vishnu to slay the asuras of tamas, she awakens the sadhaka to slay the inner asuras of mamatva and moha. (Source: Devi Mahatmya 1.44-58.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Devi called Maha-maya and Yoga-nidra in this charitra?
seoFaqs.answer: In the Prathama Charitra, Devi is invoked specifically as Yoga-nidra (the cosmic sleep) and Maha-maya (the great cosmic illusion) because the entire episode unfolds during the cosmic dissolution (mahapralaya) when only Vishnu remains, lying on Shesha in yoga-trance. That very trance is Devi herself — she is what holds Vishnu in cosmic sleep between creations. As Maha-maya she is the power that veils Brahman and projects the entire universe; as Yoga-nidra she is the power that withdraws the universe back into Brahman. Brahma's stuti establishes that creation, preservation and dissolution are all her play — tvam svaha tvam svadha tvam hi vashatkarah svaratmika, tvayaitad dharyate vishvam, tvayaitat srijyate jagat. Without her withdrawing herself from Vishnu, even the supreme Vishnu cannot act. This establishes the foundational Shakta theology that Shakti is the active principle and Brahman/Vishnu is the substratum — neither functions without the other. (Source: Devi Mahatmya 1.49-71; Devi Bhagavata.)
